quiz.question:
It's time for Black to activate their bishop. 

What's the best plan to give the bishop a good diagonal?

answers.text: Bc8, to activate it with Bf5, Bg4 or Bh3.

feedback.text:
Correct! 

The bishop can go attack the light squares on the kingside. 

The queenside is full of pawns on light squares, so the bishop isn't good there.

distractors.text: b4, to play c3 and activate the bishop with a discovered attack on the f1 rook.

feedback.text: If you play b4, White can play Qxa4. After c3, the rook can just move.

distractors.text: Bb7, to control the light squares and support the d5 pawn.

feedback.text: On b7, your bishop just stares at its own d5 pawn, without attacking or moving possibilities.
